text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, mega-cap tech squeezed shorts. QQQ ripped 1.56% with SOXX +5.87% as crowded semiconductor shorts covered after Friday’s NFP-driven flush while small caps lagged despite green prints. QQQ and SPY traded higher on 2.3x and 1.9x volume respectively, signaling active re-risking into tech/growth rather than passive drift. Oil’s 1.60% gain plus bid in XLE alongside soft Treasuries and flat credit points to a market fading rate fears and leaning back into cyclical and AI hardware upside on Iran-Israel headlines.

INDICES — VIX slipped to 18.9, below Friday but still above its 20-day 17.1, keeping macro hedges sticky. Today’s return ranking: SOXX +5.87% > QQQ +1.56% > IWM +0.87% > SPY +0.23% > DIA -0.15%, with STYLE reading LARGE > SMALL by 0.69pp. The standout cross-asset move was USO +1.60% versus TLT -0.52%, underscoring oil-up, long-duration-down reflation positioning.

VOLUME — QQQ at 2.3x and SPY at 1.9x average volume with prices up signals outright accumulation in large-cap tech and index futures. Sector and industry flow was concentrated in XLK at 1.1x and high-beta themes LIT 5.7x plus BLOK 4.0x, while defensives like XLP at 0.8x and XLI at 0.7x saw low-conviction selling.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +2.15%, XLE Energy +1.14%, XLY Discretionary +0.46%, XLV Healthcare -0.24%, XLI Industrials -0.32%, XLP Staples -0.44%, XLC Comms -0.52%, XLF Financials -0.63%, XLB Materials -1.32%, XLRE Real Estate -1.50%, XLU Utilities -1.87%. Top industries were DRAM +8.48% and WGMI +6.75%, while UTES -1.59% and REZ -1.64% anchored the downside. Money clearly flowed into semis and AI-adjacent cyclicals (SOXX +5.87%, IBIT +5.13%) and out of bond-proxy defensives including XLU -1.87% and XLRE -1.50%. This is classic AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY’s options tape showed ATM IV 18.3% with PCR_vol 1.00 and +11.8% skew, indicating balanced flow but persistent downside premium. QQQ’s PCR_vol 1.44 with ATM IV 28.8% and +16.0% skew highlights heavy protection demand even as price ripped, while VIX and VXN both trade above their 20-day averages, signaling upside chase alongside unresolved macro and tech-vol fear.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil up 1.60% with XLE +1.14% while IEF -0.11% and TLT -0.52% shows geopolitical risk expressing through crude and a mild bear-steepener rather than a flight to duration or gold. GLD’s muted 0.26% bounce alongside SLV’s 0.02% and HYG’s +0.14% bid underscores that today’s risk was about growth and AI cyclicality, not systemic credit or macro stress.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 66.1 (Greed), with MU +8.48% (DRAM), MRVL +8.98% (SOXX component), and BLOK +3.32% (Industries) leading high-beta upside. LIT’s 5.7x volume with -1.55% price is distribution in EV/battery, while BLOK’s 4.0x with +3.32% is accumulation in crypto infrastructure. EWP 5.9x on flat price and GREK 4.4x on +0.13% flag European country ETFs as quietly in play. Watch XLK +2.15% for follow-through versus recent -5.92% 5d drawdown, GLD +0.26% for whether the -8.41% 20d bleed stabilizes, and IWM +0.87% for any catch-up after SIZE stayed in BEAR regime with zscore -0.03.
